chars/4 heuristic estimator (Tier 3 fallback per egress_proxy/src/decision.rs historical heuristic).
Intentionally simple: this is the default pre-Tier-2 estimator. Production adopters should swap in SharpToken (for OpenAI models) or the sidecar's tokenizer UDS service (for non-OpenAI models). Both are tracked as follow-up work in design.md §3.3 ADR-004.

T:Spendguard.AgentFramework.Ids.IdempotencyKeyDerivation
member.summary: Idempotency-key derivation for LLM_CALL_PRE / TOOL_CALL_PRE decision boundaries, byte-compatible with the Python SDK's derive_idempotency_key helper (review-standards P1).

remarks.para: Per design.md ADR-007: idempotency_key = blake2b(canonical_concat) over (tenant_id, session_id, run_id, step_id, llm_call_id, trigger).

We use XxHash3 (built-in to System.IO.Hashing) as the runtime hash here purely for deterministic, dependency-free derivation in pre-release; the byte layout (canonical UTF-8 newline-delimited tuple then hashed) matches the Python helper. The hash function itself is scheduled to switch to BLAKE2b in a follow-up slice when the SDK dependency lands (parity test stays valid because the canonical pre-image is identical; only the digest function differs).

member.summary: Build the canonical pre-image string that gets hashed into the idempotency key. Exposed for the cross-language parity test (review-standards P1).

member.summary: Derive the idempotency-key bytes for the canonical (tenant, session, run, step, llm_call, trigger) tuple.

member.summary: Hex-encoded form of for logging / display. Reviewer Sec5: only the hex digest can appear in logs, never the pre-image.

member.name: T:Spendguard.AgentFramework.Middleware.SpendGuardChatMiddleware
member.summary:
Microsoft.Extensions.AI implementation that gates every call through the SpendGuard sidecar.
Lifecycle per design.md §3.1: 1. BEFORE next.GetResponseAsync — call RequestDecision with LLM_CALL_PRE. 2. On STOP / STOP_RUN_PROJECTION — throw . 3. On REQUIRE_APPROVAL — throw . 4. On CONTINUE / DEGRADE — call next.GetResponseAsync. 5. If the inner call throws — release the reservation. 6. AFTER next.GetResponseAsync — (Stage 2; stubbed in SLICE_04) emit LLM_CALL_POST with real usage.
DI-friendly constructor. wraps the inner ; the middleware delegates to it only after a CONTINUE / DEGRADE decision.

Sidecar signs the canonical encoding of all fields above (sidecar_version, schema_bundle, contract_bundle, capability_required, active_key_epochs, protocol_version, session_id) using the platform-issued sidecar workload key. member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ClaimEstimate
member.summary:
============================================================================ ClaimEstimate — egress_proxy / SDK supplied prediction metadata (SLICE_10). ============================================================================
All 17 prediction columns the audit chain expects per `audit-chain-prediction-extension-v1alpha1.md` §2.1+§2.2.
Field-tag layout: 1..15 — hot-path fields (single-byte varint). 16.. — additive / cold metadata.
Sentinel semantics mirror the CloudEvent extension attributes (`spendguard/common/v1/common.proto` tags 300-317): * predicted_b_tokens = 0 → Strategy B null (cache miss → L1 cold-start fall back to A as reservation) * predicted_c_tokens = 0 → Strategy C null (no plugin OR plugin returned no signal) * prediction_confidence = 0.0 → Strategy A row (NULL on SQL side) * cold_start_layer_used = "" → warm path (no cold-start fallback) * tokenizer_version_id = "" → Tier 3 fallback (heuristic, no versioned encoder) * run_predicted_remaining_steps = -1 → projector unreachable * run_steps_completed_so_far = 0 → run is at first call (cold cache) * run_code_triggered = "" → no RUN_* code emitted

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ClaimEstimate.TokenizerTier
member.summary: ── Tokenizer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Tier 2 = local exact BPE; Tier 3 = chars/4 heuristic fallback.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ClaimEstimate.ClassifierVersion
member.summary: ── Classifier + prompt-class fingerprint ────────────────────────────── Spec §8.2: fingerprint capture both predictor's view + caller's view.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ClaimEstimate.Model
member.summary: Aggregator mirror fields (canonical_events 0018). These are not common.v1 CloudEvent tag-300 fields, so sidecar copies them into the signed CloudEvent data JSON for canonical_ingest to mirror.

member.summary: Idempotency at the trigger level; sidecar may return same decision twice.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.PlannedStepsHint
member.summary:
=== SLICE_09 additive: Signal 3 pass-through ===
SDK with_run_plan decorator (SLICE_12) writes this into the request_decision metadata; sidecar passes through to run_cost_projector.Project per run-cost-projector-spec-v1alpha1.md §5.
Wire-compat per proto3 additive evolution: v1alpha1 clients send 0 (proto3 default), which the projector treats as "Signal 3 inactive" per spec §5.2. v1alpha2 clients with the SDK decorator send a positive integer naming the total planned LLM + tool steps.
Tag 17 chosen as the next stable tag after the v1alpha2 run_code_ triggered field (tag 16 on DecisionResponse). DecisionRequest tags 10-15 are reserved for future hot-path fields per common.proto §field-number convention.
SLICE_02 stubbed RUN_* code pass-through but did NOT add this field (no projector to consume it); SLICE_09 lands the field + activates the projector wire. Field cap 10000 enforced server-side at run_cost_projector validation (services/run_cost_projector/src/ server.rs MAX_PLANNED_STEPS).

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the DecisionRequest message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.Types.Inputs
member.summary: Inputs schema (per Contract §12; pre-normalized to canonical units). Hot-path: NO Platform Pricing DB query here (Stage 2 §9.4).

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.Types.Inputs.ProjectedClaims
member.summary: Estimated cost projection for this decision boundary. Sidecar normalized via cached pricing schema (in-process, < 1ms).

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.Types.Inputs.ProjectedP50Atomic
member.summary: Risk band hints (per Contract §14): p50 / p90 / p95 / p99.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionRequest.Types.Inputs.ClaimEstimate
member.summary:
=== SLICE_10 additive: ClaimEstimate enrichment ===
egress_proxy (after SLICE_10) calls tokenizer library + output_predictor + run_cost_projector BEFORE constructing this DecisionRequest, and attaches the full prediction metadata here. The sidecar reads these values into the audit_decision CloudEvent (common.v1 tags 300-313) so the audit chain carries the entire prediction story end-to-end.
Wire-compat per proto3 additive evolution: * SLICE_09 and earlier producers (SDK wrapper, legacy egress_proxy) omit this field; sidecar treats it as "estimate not supplied" and leaves the prediction CloudEvent tags at proto3 defaults (existing behaviour — Strategy A imputed locally if needed in legacy path, audit row prediction fields = NULL). * SLICE_10 egress_proxy / SLICE_12 SDK populate this field; sidecar reads the values verbatim into the CloudEvent.
Tag 8 (next free in Inputs); additive.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.MutationPatchJson
member.summary: Mutation patch (per Contract §11; RFC 6902 restricted). Only present if decision == DEGRADE.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.EffectHash
member.summary: Idempotency key for adapter.apply_mutation (per Stage 2 §4.6). Same effect_hash → same mutation; replay safe.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.LedgerTransactionId
member.summary: Reservation context — effect already reserved + audit committed.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.ApprovalRequestId
member.summary: For REQUIRE_APPROVAL: approval_request_id + ttl + approver scope.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.RunCodeTriggered
member.summary:
=== v1alpha2 additive change === Which RUN_* decision code triggered this decision, if any. One of: - "" (per-call decision; no RUN_* match) - "RUN_BUDGET_PROJECTION_EXCEEDED" (per contract-dsl-v1alpha2 §3.1) - "RUN_DRIFT_DETECTED" (per contract-dsl-v1alpha2 §3.2) - "RUN_STEPS_EXCEEDED" (per contract-dsl-v1alpha2 §3.3)
Tag 16 chosen as next available; v1alpha1 reserved tags 1-15 for hot-path fields (per common.proto §field-number convention). Wire-compat with v1alpha1 clients: proto3 default = "" (field absent), so old clients silently ignore.
SLICE_02 (this slice) wires the field through DecisionResponse but the value is only populated by SLICE_09's run_cost_projector integration. SLICE_02-time decoders should treat empty-string as "no RUN_* triggered" and not as an error.

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the DecisionResponse message type.

member.name: F:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DecisionResponse.Types.Decision.StopRunProjection
member.summary:
=== v1alpha2 additive change === Explicit decision when stop is driven by run projection, distinct from per-call STOP. Effect lattice still maps to STOP for old consumers (per Contract DSL v1alpha2 §3.4); the new field `run_code_triggered` carries the RUN_* code explicitly so SIEM / dashboard filters can distinguish "per-call stop" from "run-projection stop" without reading `reason_codes`. STOP semantics are completely identical to v1alpha1 STOP; this enum value exists for first-class dashboard categorisation.
Backwards compat (per contract-dsl-spec-v1alpha2.md §6.1 + §8.1): v1alpha1 sidecar binaries cannot emit this value (their loader refuses v1alpha2 contract bundles via capability_required mismatch). Old clients that deserialize an unknown enum value as DECISION_UNSPECIFIED must fail closed, not gracefully continue; supported SDKs map STOP_RUN_PROJECTION to the same terminal exception path as STOP. SLICE_09 lights this enum up when run_cost_projector is wired.

member.summary:
Phase 2B Step 7 (Contract §5 commit_state_machine): adapter-supplied estimated atomic amount that drives the CommitEstimated path. Mutually exclusive with `provider_reported_amount_atomic` (field 2).
* estimated_amount_atomic non-empty + outcome=SUCCESS -> sidecar calls Ledger.CommitEstimated with this amount as the estimated_amount; ledger transitions reservation 'reserved' -> 'committed' and releases the residual back to available_budget. * provider_reported_amount_atomic non-empty -> sidecar calls Ledger.ProviderReport (deferred to Step 8 in this POC; sidecar returns UNIMPLEMENTED in TraceEventAck.error). * both empty + outcome=SUCCESS -> rejected (no commit amount supplied). * both non-empty -> rejected (mutually exclusive paths). * outcome != SUCCESS -> rejected (Release path deferred; reservation will TTL-release).
NUMERIC(38,0) decimal string, same encoding as the provider-reported field. Required when the caller wants the CommitEstimated path.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.ActualInputTokens
member.summary:
Optional provider-observed token totals for audit calibration.
Proto3 optional is intentional: omitted means the producer did not observe real usage and the audit mirror MUST remain SQL NULL. A present value of 0 is an explicit provider-observed zero and is preserved by canonical_ingest / audit_outbox mirror code.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.HasActualInputTokens
member.summary: Gets whether the "actual_input_tokens" field is set

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.ClearActualInputTokens
member.summary: Clears the value of the "actual_input_tokens" field

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.HasActualOutputTokens
member.summary: Gets whether the "actual_output_tokens" field is set

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.ClearActualOutputTokens
member.summary: Clears the value of the "actual_output_tokens" field

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.LlmCallPostPayload.DeltaBRatio
member.summary: Optional calibration ratios computed by the adapter when it has both actual usage and the prediction it wants to compare. Omitted means "not applicable"; 0.0 is still treated as unset by mirror code because a zero denominator/no Strategy B-C path is the dominant sentinel in the audit-chain extension.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.IssueBudgetGrantRequest.Types.Scope.ReservationId
member.summary: Required JWT custom claim per Contract §8 grant.claims. Identifies the parent reservation backing this delegation; child agent's first ReserveSet recheck verifies budget headroom against this reservation's ledger state.

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.DrainSignal.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the DrainSignal message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest
member.summary: Field-tag layout: ASP Draft-01 §4 canonical fields hold tags 1-3 verbatim. SpendGuard extensions (tenant assertion, fencing identity) live at tags 100+ so a Draft-01-generated client wire-serializes identically. Tags 4-99 are reserved for future Draft-01 additions.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest.ReservationId
member.summary: The reservation to release. UUIDv7 from a prior ReserveResponse's reservation_ids. Released independently per-reservation: callers holding multiple reservations under one decision MUST call ReleaseReservation once per reservation_id.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest.IdempotencyKey
member.summary: Idempotency key for retries. The (reservation_id, idempotency_key) pair is the dedup key per ASP Draft-01 §3.3: - same pair + identical body → return original outcome - same pair + different body → REPLAY_CONFLICT (gRPC Status) - different idempotency_key against a terminal reservation → RESERVATION_SETTLED / RESERVATION_RELEASED (gRPC Status)

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest.ReasonCodes
member.summary: Free-form reason codes per ASP Draft-01 §4. Optional but RECOMMENDED. SpendGuard maps known values to its internal ReleaseReason enum; unknown values default to EXPLICIT. Known mappings (aligned with the EmitTraceEvents implicit path): "provider_error" | "runtime_error" | "client_timeout" → RuntimeError "run_aborted" | "run_cancelled" → RunAborted anything else → Explicit

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest.TenantId
member.summary: Tenant scope assertion. If non-empty MUST match the sidecar's configured tenant_id (permission_denied on mismatch). If empty the sidecar's own tenant_id is used implicitly. Same discipline as Handshake.tenant_id_assertion.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest.WorkloadInstanceId
member.summary: Adapter identity for fencing parity with the original Reserve. If empty, sidecar config defaults are used. Fencing epoch is checked inside run_release regardless.

member.name: T:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationResponse
member.summary: Errors are returned via gRPC Status (standard tonic / Draft-01 idiom), not embedded in the response body. Maps: ReservationStateConflict → Status::failed_precondition ReservationTtlExpired → Status::failed_precondition FencingEpochStale → Status::failed_precondition IdempotencyConflict → Status::failed_precondition (unknown reservation) → Status::not_found (tenant mismatch) → Status::permission_denied (invalid UUID) → Status::invalid_argument

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationResponse.AuditEventSignature
member.summary: Detached Ed25519 signature of the emitted audit.release CloudEvent. The full event lands in the audit chain via canonical_ingest; this signature lets the adapter pin the response to the receipt. Same- process idempotent replay returns the original signature while the sidecar replay cache is warm; cache miss returns empty bytes rather than a signature over a retry event that was not persisted.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationResponse.LedgerTransactionId
member.summary: Ledger transaction that recorded the release.

member.name: P:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationResponse.ReleasedReservationIds
member.summary: Reservations released by this call (single-element array in the current single-reservation-per-call model; kept as repeated for forward compatibility with a future batch-release form).

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.SidecarAdapter.SidecarAdapterBase.ResumeAfterApproval(Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ResumeAfterApprovalRequest,Grpc.Core.ServerCallContext)
member.summary:
Adapter calls this AFTER the human approver has approved/denied the pending approval (REQUIRE_APPROVAL outcome from RequestDecision). Sidecar: 1. Looks up the approval by (tenant_id, decision_id, approval_id). 2. Reads the current state (must be `approved` or `denied`). 3. For `approved`: re-runs Contract evaluation + Ledger.ReserveSet with a NEW idempotency key derived from approval_id (so a replay can never double-publish the effect). 4. For `denied`: emits the deny audit row + returns Decision::STOP. 5. For other states (pending/expired/cancelled): returns the typed Error code so the adapter raises the right exception.
Idempotent: repeated ResumeAfterApproval calls with the same (decision_id, approval_id) produce the same DecisionResponse because the idempotency key includes both.
member.param: The request received from the client.
The context of the server-side call handler being invoked.
member.returns: The response to send back to the client (wrapped by a task).

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.SidecarAdapter.SidecarAdapterBase.ReleaseReservation(Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest,Grpc.Core.ServerCallContext)
member.summary:
Adapter-initiated release of a held reservation before commit. Use when the provider call is aborted, the client times out, or the agent run is cancelled — and the adapter wants to surface that explicitly rather than waiting for an outcome-driven (APPLY_FAILED or trace-event-driven) implicit release.
Coexists with the implicit release paths in ConfirmPublishOutcome and EmitTraceEvents; those remain unchanged. Implicit paths are still the default for adapters that report outcomes naturally; the explicit RPC is for adapters that want to match the Agent Spend Protocol Draft-01 wire shape (docs/specs/agent-spend-protocol/draft-01.md §4).
Idempotent: repeated ReleaseReservation calls with the same (reservation_id, idempotency_key) pair return the original outcome.
member.param: The request received from the client.
The context of the server-side call handler being invoked.
member.returns: The response to send back to the client (wrapped by a task).

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.SidecarAdapter.SidecarAdapterClient.ResumeAfterApproval(Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ResumeAfterApprovalRequest,Grpc.Core.Metadata,System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Adapter calls this AFTER the human approver has approved/denied the pending approval (REQUIRE_APPROVAL outcome from RequestDecision). Sidecar: 1. Looks up the approval by (tenant_id, decision_id, approval_id). 2. Reads the current state (must be `approved` or `denied`). 3. For `approved`: re-runs Contract evaluation + Ledger.ReserveSet with a NEW idempotency key derived from approval_id (so a replay can never double-publish the effect). 4. For `denied`: emits the deny audit row + returns Decision::STOP. 5. For other states (pending/expired/cancelled): returns the typed Error code so the adapter raises the right exception.
Idempotent: repeated ResumeAfterApproval calls with the same (decision_id, approval_id) produce the same DecisionResponse because the idempotency key includes both.
member.param: The request to send to the server.
The initial metadata to send with the call. This parameter is optional.
An optional deadline for the call. The call will be cancelled if deadline is hit.
An optional token for canceling the call.
member.returns: The response received from the server.

member.name: M:Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.SidecarAdapter.SidecarAdapterClient.ReleaseReservation(Spendguard.SidecarAdapter.V1.ReleaseReservationRequest,Grpc.Core.Metadata,System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Adapter-initiated release of a held reservation before commit. Use when the provider call is aborted, the client times out, or the agent run is cancelled — and the adapter wants to surface that explicitly rather than waiting for an outcome-driven (APPLY_FAILED or trace-event-driven) implicit release.
Coexists with the implicit release paths in ConfirmPublishOutcome and EmitTraceEvents; those remain unchanged. Implicit paths are still the default for adapters that report outcomes naturally; the explicit RPC is for adapters that want to match the Agent Spend Protocol Draft-01 wire shape (docs/specs/agent-spend-protocol/draft-01.md §4).
Idempotent: repeated ReleaseReservation calls with the same (reservation_id, idempotency_key) pair return the original outcome.
member.param: The request to send to the server.
The initial metadata to send with the call. This parameter is optional.
An optional deadline for the call. The call will be cancelled if deadline is hit.
An optional token for canceling the call.
member.returns: The response received from the server.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.CommonReflection
member.summary: Holder for reflection information generated from spendguard/common/v1/common.proto

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.TraceContext.TraceState
member.summary: W3C trace state (RFC 9.4); opaque vendor-specific string.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.SpendGuardIds
member.summary:
SpendGuard canonical IDs (per Trace §3.2).
All IDs are UUID v7 (RFC 9562) for monotonic time-ordering.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef
member.summary:
Unit reference (per Contract §12.1 Money type).
Atomic amounts are interpreted relative to a unit's `scale` from Ledger `ledger_units` (e.g., USD scale=6 -> 1 USD = 1_000_000 atomic).
Canonical truth is `unit_id` (FK to ledger_units). Convenience fields below are echoed for client-side validation only; ledger derives semantics from unit_id.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.UnitName
member.summary: Token / credit / non_monetary unit_name (when not MONETARY).

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.TokenKind
member.summary: Token-kind discriminator (per Contract §12.1; required when kind == TOKEN). Examples: "input", "output", "reasoning", "cached_input", "total".

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.ModelFamily
member.summary: Model family (per Contract §12.1; required when kind == TOKEN). Tokens of different model families are NOT comparable.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.CreditProgram
member.summary: Credit program identifier (per Contract §12.1; required when kind == CREDIT).

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the UnitRef message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.UnitRef.Types.Kind
member.summary: Convenience kind for client-side validation; canonical truth is unit_id.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.Amount
member.summary:
Atomic amount as decimal string for NUMERIC(38,0) precision.
Per Ledger §3: cross-unit comparison forbidden; conversion only at explicit normalization step.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Amount.Atomic
member.summary: Decimal string, no scientific notation, no thousand separators. Example: "1000000" (= 1.000000 USD when unit.scale=6).

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay
member.summary:
Minimal replay response — returned after replay window full payload expired.
Per Ledger §7: contains no PII / full prompts / encryption keys; only status code, timestamps, and compact resource references.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.OperationId
member.summary: Operation-specific compact reference; e.g., reservation_set_id for ReserveSet, refund_credit_event_id for RefundCredit.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.DecisionId
member.summary: ORIGINAL decision_id from the first call (per Contract §6 / Ledger §7 idempotency — same idempotency_key MUST surface the same decision_id across retries). Sidecar uses this on the Replay branch to anchor its response to the original decision, not its locally-minted retry value. Not PII; a server-minted UUID7 identifier.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.ProjectionIds
member.summary: ORIGINAL projection-side identifiers from the first call, IN CANONICAL ORDER (matching the original Success response order — i.e., claim ordinal). For ReserveSet these are the reservation_ids; for other operations operation-specific (empty for ones that have no per-row projection ids). Required so the adapter sees the same set of resource handles, in the same order, across retries. Not PII; server-minted UUIDs.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.TtlExpiresAt
member.summary: ORIGINAL TTL anchor from the first call. For ReserveSet, this is the reservation's ttl_expires_at (the wallclock-derived expiry stored at first-success). The sidecar mirrors this back to the adapter on Replay so that the loser of a concurrent same-key race observes the same TTL as the winner instead of recomputing locally and returning a different wallclock value.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the Replay message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.Replay.Types.StatusCode
member.summary: Status code retained in minimal replay (per Ledger §7 minimal_replay_response). For terminal states this conveys the final disposition; for in-progress operations this conveys the latest known posting_state code.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.FullResponsePayload
member.summary:
Full response payload — first-success-only; subject to retention policy.
Per Ledger §7: tenant-policy retention (default 24h, max 30d Phase 1).

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.FullResponsePayload.Payload
member.summary: Operation-specific full payload; opaque protobuf Any encoded here.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.Error.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the Error message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.BudgetClaim.Types
member.summary: Container for nested types declared in the BudgetClaim message type.

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.LockOrderToken
member.summary:
Server derives if omitted; validates if provided.
Derivation: sorted_tuple = sort_lex_by(claims, key=(budget_id, unit.unit_id)) canonical = ",".join(f"{c.budget_id}:{c.unit.unit_id}" for c in sorted_tuple) token = "v1:" + hex(sha256(canonical))

member.name: T:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent
member.summary:
CloudEvents 1.0 envelope (structured mode).
`data` is opaque payload bytes; type-specific schema lives in canonical schema bundle (Trace §12).

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.Type
member.summary: Event type, e.g.: spendguard.audit.decision spendguard.audit.outcome spendguard.ledger.reservation spendguard.ledger.commit spendguard.ledger.release spendguard.approval.requested spendguard.approval.granted spendguard.approval.denied spendguard.approval.expired spendguard.rollback spendguard.tombstone spendguard.refund.credit_received spendguard.dispute.requested spendguard.dispute.granted spendguard.dispute.denied spendguard.dispute.withdrawn spendguard.dispute.resolved spendguard.region_failover_promoted spendguard.ledger.invoice_reconciled spendguard.ledger.reconciliation_run

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.Id
member.summary: event_id (UUID v7 string); required to be globally unique for dedupe.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.Datacontenttype
member.summary: Per Trace §7.5 CloudEvents 1.0 envelope: canonical encoding is "application/json" for the `data` field. The `data` field is bytes here (proto3 limitation); content MUST be a JSON document encoded UTF-8 unless `datacontenttype` advertises a different encoding.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.TenantId
member.summary: SpendGuard extension attributes (per Trace §7.5 spendguard.* extensions).

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.PredictionConfidence
member.summary: 0.0-1.0; absent = column-NULL on Strategy A row (see audit-chain-prediction-extension-v1alpha1.md §6.3 + round-2 fix M11). Producers MUST NOT use 0.0 as a sentinel here — calibration-report filters `WHERE prediction_confidence IS NOT NULL`.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.PredictionSampleSize
member.summary: Round-3 fix M3: int32 → int64 to match audit_outbox.prediction_sample_size (BIGINT) and audit_outbox.predicted_b_tokens (BIGINT). Eliminates the 2^31 overflow surface on multi-year aggregations without a CHECK constraint workaround. Per round-2 fix M4 the SQL side is already BIGINT — this aligns the wire to match.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.RunProjectionAtDecisionAtomic
P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.RunPredictedRemainingSteps
member.summary: -1 sentinel for unreachable; INT (not BIGINT) on SQL side matches int32 here

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.RunStepsCompletedSoFar
member.summary: Round-4 fix M1: int32 → int64 to match audit_outbox.run_steps_completed_so_far (BIGINT per round-2 fix M4 anticipating multi-year aggregation). Wire- compatible with the round-3 int32 encoding because non-negative values use identical varint wire bytes per proto3 spec; tag 313 therefore stays at 313 without a version bump.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.CloudEvent.ActualInputTokens
member.summary: Commit-side actual columns (4 per spec §2.3). Only on .outcome events.

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Idempotency.Key
member.summary: Caller-supplied idempotency key; scoped to (tenant_id, operation_kind).

member.name: P:Spendguard.Common.V1.Idempotency.RequestHash
member.summary: sha256 of canonical request body for replay-on-retry consistency check.
